home.title: Your modules
home.empty_description: No tools unlocked yet — your care team is on it.

crisis_plan.title: My plan for tough times
crisis_plan.subtitle: This plan is here to help when things get too hard.
crisis_plan.step_1_title: When I feel things going wrong
crisis_plan.step_3_title: What takes my mind off things
crisis_plan.anchors_subtitle: Photos and a phrase that remind you why it's worth it.
crisis_plan.anchor_phrase_placeholder: A phrase that brings you back to what matters…
crisis_plan.coping_cards_subtitle: Strategies your practitioner prepared just for you.
crisis_plan.coping_cards_empty: Your practitioner hasn't added any cards yet.
crisis_plan.commitment_phrase_default: I commit to using this plan before any act of self-harm, and to calling my practitioner if I don't feel able to.
crisis_plan.urgency_no_items: No contacts filled in your plan.

beck_columns.title: Untangling my thoughts
beck_columns.intro: Write down a negative thought and we'll break it down together.
beck_columns.entry_col_1_hint: Where were you? What was happening? Who was there?
beck_columns.entry_col_3_hint: What thought crossed your mind at that moment?
beck_columns.entry_col_3_placeholder: E.g. I'm useless, this is going to go badly...
beck_columns.entry_col_3_belief: How much did you believe it?
beck_columns.entry_col_4_hint: Another way to see this? What facts contradict the thought?
beck_columns.entry_col_5_hint: How do you feel now, after working through this?
beck_columns.entry_col_5_belief: How much do you believe this new thought?
beck_columns.empty_alert_msg: Fill in at least the situation or the automatic thought.
beck_columns.footer: Cognitive restructuring journal (Beck's cognitive therapy). Hofmann et al., Cognit Ther Res 2012 · NICE NG222 (2022).

grounding.title: Back to the present
grounding.intro: When everything feels overwhelming, this technique brings you back.

mood_tracker.title: How are you doing?
mood_tracker.intro: Tell me how you're feeling today.
mood_tracker.instructions: Rate each dimension of your mood right now.
mood_tracker.empty_text: Record your first entry to see your history.
mood_tracker.footer: Your numbers are private and reviewed with your therapist.
mood_tracker.reminder_none: No reminder set by your therapist.
mood_tracker.markers_empty: No markers in this period. Mark an event (medication change, life event…) to place it on your charts.
mood_tracker.markers_placeholder: E.g.: started lithium, time off work…
mood_tracker.month_legend: The higher the day's average score, the darker the circle (1 = light, 10 = dark).

fear_thermometer.title: My stress level
fear_thermometer.intro: Rate your stress right now (0 = super calm, 100 = total panic).
fear_thermometer.empty_text: Tap the button below to log your first stress level.
fear_thermometer.situations_hint: Name the moments that stress you out so you can pick them again later.
fear_thermometer.suds_hint_after: Fill in when it's done. You can come back later.
fear_thermometer.situation_missing_msg: Say what stressed you before saving.
fear_thermometer.situation_catalogue_empty: No situations saved. Add some from the main screen.
fear_thermometer.footer: The SUDS scale measures your stress level — used in exposure therapy. Wolitzky-Taylor et al. 2008 · Craske et al. 2014.
fear_thermometer.disclaimer: This module supports your appointments. The techniques shown were discussed with your care provider — it doesn't replace your care.
fear_thermometer.ladder_empty_text: Add the situations that stress you out, from easiest to hardest.
fear_thermometer.step_label_placeholder: E.g. taking the subway when it's packed
fear_thermometer.detail_no_sessions: No exposure yet. Go for it when you feel ready.
fear_thermometer.expectation_placeholder: What am I afraid will happen?
fear_thermometer.step_target_hint: How stressful is this situation before you start working on it? Used to order your ladder, from easiest to hardest.

decisional_balance.title: The pros and cons
decisional_balance.intro: Not sure? Let's lay it all out to see things clearly.
decisional_balance.quadrant_cons_status_title: What staying the same costs me

sleep_diary.title: My night journal
sleep_diary.intro: Log each morning how you slept to spot patterns over time.
sleep_diary.awakenings_label: How many times you woke up
sleep_diary.quality_missing_msg: Rate the quality of your night (the stars).
sleep_diary.footer: CBT-I night journal — evidence-based technique for insomnia. Trauer et al., Ann Intern Med 2015 · NICE NG215 (2022).

medication_side_effects.label: Treatment side effects tracker
medication_side_effects.title: My treatment effects
medication_side_effects.intro: Feeling weird effects from your meds? Log them here.
medication_side_effects.footer: Raw figures you enter. To look at with your clinician.
medication_side_effects.empty_text: Save your first entry to see the tracking.
medication_side_effects.events_empty: Your clinician hasn't added any notes yet.
medication_side_effects.instructions: Rate how strong each effect is right now.
medication_side_effects.markers_empty: No markers in this period. Mark a treatment event (new medication, dose change…) to place it on your charts.
medication_side_effects.markers_placeholder: E.g. switched to 150 mg, new medication…
medication_side_effects.reminder_none: No reminder set by your clinician.
medication_side_effects.history_empty: Tracking will show up after your first entries.
medication_side_effects.custom_placeholder: Name of the effect to track…
medication_side_effects.config_hint: Pick the effects that concern you. You can also add a custom one.
medication_side_effects.config_empty_text: Tap “Configure my effects” to choose what to track.

medication_adherence.title: My medication log
medication_adherence.intro: Did you take your medication this week?
medication_adherence.calendar_legend: Filled day — colour of the status you picked
medication_adherence.meds_empty: You haven't added any medication yet.

behavioral_activation.title: My fun activities
behavioral_activation.intro: Let's find activities that make you feel good and plan them.
behavioral_activation.empty_text:
Add an activity you're planning or that you did.
Rate it for Pleasure (P) and Mastery (M).
behavioral_activation.month_hint_tap: Tap a day to see your activities
behavioral_activation.activity_placeholder: E.g.: 20-min walk, Call a friend, Reading…

breathing_techniques.title: Breathing to calm down
breathing_techniques.intro: Pick an exercise — it can really help you come down.

rim.title: Rewriting the nightmare
rim.intro: We'll turn your nightmare into something better.
rim.empty_text:
Your care team hasn't written your alternative scenario yet.
It'll be here after your next session.
rim.disclaimer: Use this with your care team's support only.
rim.sounds_hint: Play a sound while you read. (Coming soon)
rim.protocol_step_1: Get comfy somewhere quiet, ideally lying down.
rim.protocol_step_2: Take a few slow, deep breaths before you start.
rim.protocol_step_3: Read your alternative scenario once or twice — take your time.
rim.protocol_step_4: Close your eyes and picture the images from the scenario.
rim.protocol_step_5: Practise every evening, ideally just before bed.

cognitive_saturation.title: Stop the rumination
cognitive_saturation.intro: This technique cuts through thoughts that keep looping.

emotion_wheel.title: What I really feel
emotion_wheel.intro: Put precise words to what you're feeling, so you can spot it and talk about it with your therapist.
emotion_wheel.empty_text: Tap the button at the top to spot your first emotion.
emotion_wheel.step_primary_hint: Pick the emotion that comes closest.
emotion_wheel.step_secondary_hint: Pick the shade that fits you best.
emotion_wheel.step_specific_hint: Refine it with a more exact word.
emotion_wheel.step_intensity_hint: From 1 (very low) to 10 (very high). Raw value.
emotion_wheel.step_notes_hint: Context, situation, related thought… Free text.
emotion_wheel.footer: Emotion wheel — putting words to your feelings actually helps. Lieberman et al., Psychol Sci 2007 · Kircanski et al., Psychol Sci 2012.

phq9.empty_text: Tap the button to get started.
phq9.instructions_2: how much have these things been bothering you?
phq9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9.q6: Feeling bad about yourself or like you've let people down
phq9.q7: Trouble concentrating on things like reading or watching something
phq9.q8: Moving or speaking really slowly, or being so restless you can't sit still
phq9.q9: Thoughts that you'd be better off dead or of hurting yourself
phq9.footer: Your score goes to your clinician — they interpret it.

bsl23.empty_text: Tap the button to get started.
bsl23.instructions_2: how much have these things been bothering you?
bsl23.q3: Feeling uncomfortable in my own skin
bsl23.q5: Feeling like I'm outside my own body
bsl23.q8: Not being able to express what I feel
bsl23.q9: Thinking about hurting or injuring myself
bsl23.q10: Feeling as though I don't exist
bsl23.q12: Feeling so bad that I want to hurt myself
bsl23.q13: Feeling so tense that I feel like I'm going to explode
bsl23.q14: Feeling agitated and unable to calm down
bsl23.q18: Finding it hard to believe that others respect me
bsl23.q20: Feeling angry and irritable for no reason
bsl23.q21: Feeling that others see me negatively
bsl23.footer: Your score goes to your clinician — they interpret it.

gad7.empty_text: Tap the button to get started.
gad7.instructions_2: how much have these things been bothering you?
gad7.q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
gad7.q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
gad7.q3: Worrying too much about different things
gad7.q5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
gad7.q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen
gad7.footer: Your score goes to your clinician — they interpret it.

snap_iv.instructions_1: Adult evaluation — to be filled in by a parent or teacher.
snap_iv.instructions_2: For each of the following behaviours, indicate how often you observe it in the child.
snap_iv.warning: This questionnaire must be completed by a parent, guardian or teacher — not by the child.
snap_iv.q1: Fails to give close attention to details or makes careless mistakes.
snap_iv.q2: Has difficulty sustaining attention in tasks or play.
snap_iv.q3: Does not seem to listen when spoken to directly.
snap_iv.q4: Does not follow through on instructions and fails to finish tasks.
snap_iv.q5: Has difficulty organising tasks and activities.
snap_iv.q6: Avoids or dislikes tasks requiring sustained mental effort.
snap_iv.q7: Loses things necessary for tasks or activities.
snap_iv.q10: Fidgets with hands or feet or squirms in seat.
snap_iv.q11: Leaves seat when remaining seated is expected.
snap_iv.q12: Runs about or climbs excessively when inappropriate.
snap_iv.q14: Is 'on the go' or acts as if 'driven by a motor'.
snap_iv.q16: Blurts out answers before questions are completed.
snap_iv.q21: Actively defies or refuses to comply with adults' requests.
snap_iv.q23: Blames others for mistakes or misbehaviour.
snap_iv.q24: Is touchy or easily annoyed by others.
snap_iv.footer: The score goes to the clinician — they interpret it.

asrs6.instructions_2: for each question, pick the answer that best fits you.
asrs6.q1: How often do you fail to wrap up the final details of a project once the hard parts are done?
asrs6.q2: How often do you have trouble getting organised when a task needs it?
asrs6.q3: How often do you forget appointments or things you need to do?
asrs6.q4: When something needs a lot of thought, how often do you put off getting started?
asrs6.q5: How often do you fidget or squirm when you have to sit still for a long time?
asrs6.q6: How often do you feel like you're running on overdrive or can't slow down?
asrs6.footer: Your score goes to your clinician — they interpret it.

asrs18.instructions_2: for each question, pick the answer that best fits you.
asrs18.q1: How often do you fail to wrap up the final details of a project once the hard parts are done?
asrs18.q2: How often do you have trouble getting organised when a task needs it?
asrs18.q3: How often do you forget appointments or things you need to do?
asrs18.q4: When something needs a lot of thought, how often do you put off getting started?
asrs18.q5: How often do you fidget or squirm when you have to sit still for a long time?
asrs18.q6: How often do you feel like you're running on overdrive or can't slow down?
asrs18.q7: How often do you make careless mistakes on boring or difficult tasks?
asrs18.q8: How often do you struggle to stay focused on repetitive work?
asrs18.q9: How often do you struggle to concentrate on what someone is saying, even when they're talking directly to you?
asrs18.q10: How often do you misplace or lose things at home or at work?
asrs18.q11: How often are you distracted by things happening around you?
asrs18.q12: How often do you leave your seat in situations where staying seated is expected?
asrs18.q13: How often do you feel restless or fidgety?
asrs18.q14: How often do you have trouble winding down when you have free time?
asrs18.q15: How often do you talk too much in social situations?
asrs18.q16: How often do you finish people's sentences before they can finish them?
asrs18.q17: How often do you struggle to wait your turn?
asrs18.q18: How often do you interrupt others when they're busy?
asrs18.footer: Your score goes to your clinician — they interpret it.

rcads.instructions: For each of the following statements, please indicate how often this happens for you.
rcads.footer: This questionnaire is a monitoring tool. Only your practitioner can interpret the results.
rcads.q1: I worry when I think I have done poorly at something.
rcads.q2: I feel scared that something bad will happen to me.
rcads.q3: I worry about being as good as other kids.
rcads.q5: I am scared of going to school.
rcads.q6: Suddenly I feel as if I can't breathe when there is no reason for this.
rcads.q9: Suddenly I feel really scared for no reason at all.
rcads.q10: I don't want to be away from my family.
rcads.q11: I feel scared to sleep away from home.
rcads.q12: I worry when someone is late.
rcads.q13: I don't want to go to school because I miss my parents.
rcads.q14: I feel scared that something bad will happen to someone in my family.
rcads.q15: I feel nervous when I have to perform in front of people.
rcads.q16: I feel scared that I will do something embarrassing in front of people.
rcads.q17: I worry about what other people think of me.
rcads.q18: I feel scared when I have to talk in front of the class.
rcads.q19: I have unpleasant thoughts that keep coming back.
rcads.q20: I have to keep doing things over and over (like washing my hands, cleaning, or putting things in order) so that bad things won't happen.
rcads.q21: I don't enjoy things as much as before.
rcads.q23: I feel tired or lack energy.
rcads.q25: I have lost interest in things I used to enjoy.

nsi.instructions: For each question, choose the number that best matches your experience over the past month (0 = least / 5 = most):
nsi.pct_question: 10. What percentage of your nightmares are recurring?
nsi.themes_question: 11. What are the recurring theme(s) of your nightmares?
nsi.footer: The score is shared with your care provider. Interpretation is their responsibility.

epds.label: EPDS
epds.instructions: You have recently had a baby. We would like to know how you are feeling. Choose the answer which best describes how you have felt in the past 7 days.
epds.footer: Your score is shared with your healthcare provider. Its interpretation is their responsibility.
epds.empty_text: Tap the button to get started.

motivational_balance.label: Motivational balance
motivational_balance.description: Understanding your ambivalence and what drives change
motivational_balance.disclaimer: This module supports your consultations. It was introduced with your clinician — it doesn't replace your therapeutic follow-up.
motivational_balance.stage_subtitle: Pick the stage that best describes you today.
motivational_balance.stage_1_desc: I don't see a reason to change
motivational_balance.stage_2_desc: I'm thinking about it, but haven't made up my mind
motivational_balance.stage_6_desc: I've gone back to the old habit — that's totally normal
motivational_balance.rulers_importance_q: How important is it for you to change?
motivational_balance.rulers_importance_why_label: Why that score and not lower?
motivational_balance.rulers_confidence_q: If you decided to change, how confident would you feel?
motivational_balance.rulers_confidence_why_label: What would make you more confident?
motivational_balance.rulers_confidence_why_placeholder: What I'd need to believe in it…
motivational_balance.rulers_commitment_placeholder: In one sentence, what I'm taking away from today…
motivational_balance.balance_values_subtitle: Choose up to 3 values — they'll anchor your balance.
motivational_balance.balance_commitment_placeholder: In one sentence, what I'm taking away from today…

cape42.label: CAPE-42 — Psychotic Experiences
cape42.description: Self-assessment of unusual perceptions and beliefs
